items.caption:
If your boss doesn’t f with you… it’s game over!

And you need to find a new way out ASAP. 

P.S. Our book comes out SOON and you can get all the pre-order bonuses + free coaching when you comment SUMMER! Or go to link workhap.com/book. ✅ 

#career #jobsearch #badboss
items.transcript: Nobody's going to tell you this, but you can never outwork a boss who doesn't like you. I don't care if you show up early. I don't care if you stay late. I don't care if you're the best damn person on your team. If your boss already has it in their mind that they don't like you and they don't want you on the team, you will never, ever change their mind. I think one of the best skills you can learn in corporate is not just learning who you want to work with, but also understanding who wants to work with you. The hard truth is not everyone's going to like you. Because I've learned there's really only a handful of people in your career who can make or break your career, and by far the number one person who has the most impact is going to be your boss. So if you can already feel from their energy that they don't really like you, they don't want to see you succeed, they'd rather have anybody else do better than you, you need to take that as a sign and leave as soon as you get those signs. I've seen way too many careers wasted. If you're in that moment right now, just know it's never been about you and it's always on them. For you, it still means better for you to go somewhere else than to stay here and try to change something that will never change. That's my two cents. What would you do if your boss didn't like you? Let me know.

Companies are getting busted for tracking employee data!

What would you do if your company asked you to train AI?

P.S. Last day for 25% off on Barnes & Noble for our book! Use code: PREORDER25 and go to link in profile!

#career #ai
items.transcript: If you think your company's not tracking your employee surveys for how you work, you've got to read this article because Meta in their AI training program were already recording the keystrokes of their employees, but it turns out they were also recording all of these things and other sensitive information. We're in a time right now where companies are actively trying to recruit employees to train their AI models to help their consumers, which to a certain degree does make sense because you do need people to train technology. But what happens when the people who are training the technology ends up training it to the point where it can replace their job? Now that's the scary thing. You're damned if you do, you're damned if you don't. If you do it, you're pretty much giving up your privacy and giving everything to the company. But if you don't do it, then your company can look at you and say, well, we need people to train, and if you can't do it, then we're going to find somebody else. I think it's one of the hardest times to be a tech employee right now, but in my two cents, I think you've got to play the game here. You have to do the things that your company wants while still maintaining your privacy and understanding that you have to protect yourself from all this that's happening. What would you do if you got asked to train AI? Let me know in the comments.

items.caption:
Please remember this the next time you think you’re a “bad networker”

Because it’s not YOU! It’s the game. And it’s awk and weird and new for everyone. 

That’s at least how I felt here at Cannes this week and I’ve done this a lot already!

Do you get the ick when it comes to networking? What holds you back!

P.S. Our book is on SALE until tomorrow with code: PREORDER on Barnes and Noble with link in profile! ✅ 

#career #networking
items.transcript: I think the biggest lie that we tell people is that introverts have it easier for networking than extroverts. That is not true. I'm here in Kansas week and I'm going to all these different events and meeting people and doing all these things and it is so hard. And for me, I'm an extrovert. This is what my Myers-Briggs looks like. And I still find it hard to have the energy to meet people and go out there and connect. So I think we're lying to people saying that if you are an introvert that you can have it easier or even if you're an extrovert that it can be easy. Networking is a skill on its own. And as somebody who is decent at it, someone who does it a lot, the best advice I can give you if you're scared of all these things is that everybody is scared. I do this all the time and I'm so scared. Most of the time this is what I look like, just sitting in the corner and waiting for something to happen. But really the only way to get better is to try and put yourself out there, meet other people who are kind of like in your shoes. But one thing I want you to remember is that everyone else is feeling it too. Everyone who you think is the most confident and social and extra person in the world, they're also nervous. And the more you can remember that, the easier it will be for you to reach out to them. Okay?

items.caption:
The biggest red flags you gotta avoid! 🚩 

Our book is on sale now on Barnes & Noble for 25% off until Friday! Pre-order with code: PREORDER25 and get all the bonuses that expire on June 30!

#career #badjob
items.transcript: You know a job is going to be straight bullshit when they hire you immediately. He's not wrong, and it's not because you are the best candidate ever and they need to have you, it's because they can't find anybody else and they're desperate. Let me tell about other red flags. If the company doesn't really give you a role, they're just like, yeah, just come in here, you'll find a spot, no worries. That's code for you're going to be wearing so many different hats. We don't even have a role for you because you're going to be doing 10 different ones. At first, they're going to try to sell you exposure and leadership opportunities and stretch roles, but all of that means you're going to be overworked with no real direction. Another red flag you just got to look out for is seeing how long everybody else has been on the team. Because if you join and you also see another two-weeker here, someone who's been here for a month, somebody who's about to leave, all of that are glaring red flags that this is not a good place for you. Above all, you can just feel it in your body. If they're using urgency tactics on you, that is a no-go.

🌶️ Hot take: being the best on your team is the WORST spot in corporate. 

The sweet spot is to be the SECOND best on your team. 

The number 1 has to deal with all the urgencies and politics that come with it. The number 2 can just do their job and go home.

You still get paid, you get still recognized, and your whole life doesn’t have to be centered around the job. Now that’s the win.

Stop focusing on busy work and start focusing on what gets you PAID!

To advance in your career, you don’t want to just put your head down and work.

The real key is understanding the business challenges and making your manager look good!

And you can do that by using @thequillbot to showcase your great skills + insights in the form of my fav way… decks!

If you want to get ahead in your career, start working smarter with Quillbot for free!

#Quillbot #QuillbotEducator #AD
items.transcript: This is why you're not getting promoted, and I've been there too, but this is how to fix it. During my consulting days, I made the mistake of just doing busy work and not really understanding what my manager needed from me. So if I could go back in time, I'd focus less on just hitting my to-do list and more time on understanding what the business challenges are and how to make my manager look good. That's how you get recognized and promoted in your career. So then how do you do this? You want to build a habit of building decks and insights and sending it off to your manager consistently, and you can do it all for free with Quillbot. Go to Quillbot, hit create, and hit a prompt like this. Help me build an LMD deck to present to my manager to recommend a four-week sprint to build a manager training program for the client. And just like that, you have a deck that you can then present to your manager that showcases you're not just someone who takes notes, but you're someone who takes action, and that's what they want to see. So try Quillbot for free and use it to work smarter. Good luck!

The real secret to getting ahead is having bosses like this!

And as someone who lived in NYC for 3 years… GO KNICKS BABY (but I’ll be lying if I said I didn’t want the series to go to a Game 7 😝)

Tell me — have you ever had a boss who would vouch for you to get a new job? They are the best!

#career #mikebrown #knicks
items.transcript:
If you're lucky to have a manager like this, you are winning. Watch this.

Christian, my associate head coach, I am surprised he has not gotten an interview. He won the summer league championship this past summer. Somebody needs to give him an interview because he will help your team win.

It just goes to show that these days, you applying everywhere is not going to get you the job that you really want. You gotta be networking and you gotta have people who vouch for you to get noticed. This is why you having a personal brand or people talking about you or you getting in rooms that you normally wouldn't, that is often the X factor for you landing the job. It's not just going to be you hitting apply and you waiting for results. You gotta go and take it. And even better yet, if you have managers or mentors who can do that for you because they have higher pull. That's how you get results in 2026. Good luck.

#career #salary #newjob
items.transcript: This is something that a lot of people forget, but you're always going to get paid more if you go to a competitor than staying at the same company. I talk about this in the book. Let me explain why. A lot of times, HR always has a band for how much they can give raises internally. But on the flip side, if they're going to be courting somebody else who's coming in from someone new, they have a different band that they can offer them. If you've been at the same company for two, three, four, five years and someone new comes in, oftentimes you'll see that this new person comes in at a higher bracket than what you're on. Even though your experience can be the same, the title can be the same, all of it can be the same. If you are ever in that position, you can go to your boss and say, I expect more and ask for more. But you also don't want to throw this person under the bus and say, I know that this person makes more. There's a tact to it. I've found that if you really do want to get paid, you have to use a leveraging competing offer to get an offer here or just to jump ship. But again, we talk all about that and how to get paid and negotiate tips in my book, which you can pre-order here. Good luck.

items.caption:
Level up your resume game with this!

As you know, you only get 6 seconds to make an impression on your resume.

Most people miss out not because they lack experience, but because they don’t know how to showcase it the right way.

And this is how you go from a meh resume to a winning resume template using @thequillbot for free!

Try this prompt in Quillbot:

“Create a resume for [Role] highlighting my background in [Skill 1], [Skill 2], and [Skill 3]. Create it in a standard clean doc with simple structure and headings in this order: summary, key skills, experience, and education.”

Use that to make a splash and land that job! 

#Quillbot #QuillbotEducator #ad
items.transcript: Six seconds is all you get on your resume, so you gotta make them count. And as an ex-recruiter, I'm telling you, the reason why people get rejected on their resume is not because they don't have the right experience, but they don't know how to sell themselves the right way. That's what I want to teach you today with the three T's, how to make them tailored, timely, and tight. Let's go. So instead of bad resumes like these, this is the template that I recommend, especially for career pivoters and people who want to level up in their career. And I'm going to show you how you can do this with Quillbot like this. Use this prompt. Create a resume for a learning and development role, highlighting my background in training, analytics, and HR. Create it in a standard clean doc with simple structure and headings in this order. Summary, key skills, experience, and education. And just like that, you get this, which you can then tailor and make it your own to really make a splash. So then you just went from this to this, a winning resume all with Quillbot for free. Good luck.

items.caption:
🌶️ I say do NOT fall into this trap! 🫣 

I know AI is hot and you want to learn the latest and greatest but…

It’s so much more effective to actually do what you want vs just jumping on the biggest trend just for the sake of it. 

I would much rather see you pursue skills and careers you are genuinely interested in! That will serve you so much better for the long run. 

What are your thoughts on this hot take?

#career #ai #hottake
items.transcript: No one's going to tell you this, but I think the biggest trend in 2026 is getting a master's in AI. If you do a quick Google search for AI masters, you're going to see all these programs pop up. Ivy League schools, state schools, these certifications. Doesn't that mean AI is the hardest skill to learn right now? Technically, yes, but I feel like it's you during the gold rush getting a master's in how to use a shovel versus just learning how to use a shovel. What a lot of people also forget is that these universities, I mean, they operate like businesses. Their main goal is to get you in and to teach you, not necessarily to teach you how to land a job in that industry. So of course, if they have this shiny new thing, they're like, yeah, get this AI master's, more students are going to flock to it. Now I'm not hating on all college because obviously education is important, but I think if you revolve your career always around that next hot skill, it doesn't set you up for success. Instead, you've got to build your career on stuff that you want to do and that you're good at so that you're not just chasing this shiny thing every single time. There's going to be another skill that comes around that's going to take the thunder. And are you going to get a master's in that too? I don't think so. Build the career that you want and let AI help you, but don't go all in on AI. Again, just my opinion.

items.caption:
Doing THIS will be the reason why you get ahead! 

Think about it. When did you ever really do it all alone? 

It’s rare because the best projects are a TEAM EFFORT.

And you can lead that charge by creating a Shared PDF Space in @adobeacrobat!

So the next time you’re owning a big project, upload all your docs and notes, and then give your team access to add their own!

When it’s time for the final recommendation, AI Assistant can take in all the feedback and notes, and help you give your best stuff to your boss.

Now that’s the way to do it! 

Check out Acrobat PDF Spaces! 

#AdobeAcrobatPartner #Career
items.transcript: I'm going to tell you something your boss won't. If you want to get ahead in your career, you've got to be the playmaker on your team. Not just the one who puts their heads down and works in a silo, but the one who makes decisions off of everyone's inputs and connects them all together. Let's say you're building a new product roadmap. Back in the day, you'd be done with sharing hundreds of emails and sharing notes, but today you can do it all in one place, and I'm partnering with Adobe Acrobat to show you how. And you start by creating a shared PDF space in Adobe Acrobat. This is where you upload your strategy deck, meeting notes, and input from your team. Then you'll have it all in one spot, ready to be dissected and analyzed. Share this access to everyone on your team and let them add their notes to keep things streamlined. They won't see your chat history, but it's a new, improved shared working space for teams. Now here's where you really make your mark, because you can then ask an assistant specific questions to help build your project recommendations. Like, what are some key learnings that could be implemented in future projects? And show me the data points to support our new product. An AI assistant only pulls its answers from the relevant information in your documents. You can even click the citation to show exactly where it gathered the information or save a response as a note if you want to share with your full team reference. So now when you present it to your boss, you're not just an individual contributor, you're a playmaker connecting the whole team together. Try out shared PDF spaces in Adobe Acrobat and be the one on your team who puts it all together. Check out the LinkedIn profile. Good luck!

items.caption:
Your job is KILLING you!

Comment SUMMER to join our free job search mastery classes and preorder our book!

Your job sucks and I want you to do something about it, my friend. 

If you got the Sunday scaries right now…. watch this and see which one applies. 

And to learn the real remedy to get out of a job and into a job you love, comment SUMMER to preorder our Get Hired book and join all our free bonus classes!

Intro by alexandraromanello

#career #newjob #sundayscaries
items.transcript: The biggest sign that you're in the wrong job is when you think about your job and see how your body reacts to it. Because there's actually truth behind the Sunday Scaries. If you're already dreading the week ahead and your body closes up and you don't want to do anything and anxiety takes over, you're in the wrong job. What I found after coaching thousands of people with WorkApp, there's really only three criteria that you need to hit off to enjoy the work you do. The first one is, do I even like the job that I'm doing? Not if I'm good at it, but if I like it. The second one is, do I like the people that I'm doing with? So this is your boss, their boss, your team members, all of it. And the third thing is, do I like the life that it gives me? So money, work-life balance, your vacation, do you like that? If the answer is a yes, yes, yes, I will guarantee you that your body will notice a difference. But if there's a gap, if maybe you don't like the job, but you like the people, and you don't like the work-life balance and the pay, then something needs to give. You need to find a different role, maybe at the same company, and get that pay bump. But if all three are X's, then your body is going to tell you, and you're not going to want to do anything. So it's Sunday right now, I want you to think about it for yourself. How do you feel when it comes to work, and how does your body feel about it? That's the real answer if you should get a new job or not. Good luck.
